entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogea L., the crop studied for its transpiration response to vapor pressure deficit

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate of water loss from the plant, which varies among peanut genotypes in response to VPD

entities.name: VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: Atmospheric condition that influences stomata conductance and transpiration rates

entities.name: STOMATA CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The rate of gas exchange through stomata, which decreases under high VPD

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A state of insufficient soil water available for the plant

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to maintain productivity or survive under water deficit conditions

entities.name: SOIL WATER CONSERVATION
entities.description: The reduction of soil water loss to enhance crop tolerance to water deficit

entities.name: SANDY SOILS
entities.description: Soil type characterized by low water-holding capacity

entities.name: WATER-HOLDING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to retain water, which is low in sandy soils

relationships.description: genotypes of peanut exhibit variation in their transpiration rates
transpiration rates respond to changes in vapor pressure deficit
stomata conductance decreases under high vapor pressure deficit
decreased stomata conductance leads to soil water conservation
soil water conservation is an approach for enhanced water deficit tolerance
peanut is frequently affected by water deficit
peanut is frequently grown on sandy soils
sandy soils are characterized by low water-holding capacity
low water-holding capacity contributes to water deficit
